svg.title: Shibui scope boundary — what it is, what it is not, what still needs to be built
svg.desc: Three zones arranged vertically. Top zone: what exists outside Shibui's scope — off-chain institutional processes and governance frameworks. Middle zone with amber border: what Shibui is — the credential format standard on EAS. The critical gap between them is the attestation bridge not yet built. Bottom zone: what plugs in downstream — token interfaces and agent protocols.
svg.text: NOT SHIBUI — pre-existing. must exist. Shibui depends on these but owns none of them.

g.text: the "supra on-chain entity" that carries institutional trust from off-chain to on-chain
event listener · schema mapper · key management · gas relay · ZK adapter (future)

svg.text: SHIBUI — the credential format standard. this is what exists today.

g.text: EAS attestation schema — open, EEA-maintained, chain-agnostic
isVerified(wallet) · addTrustedAttester(address, topics[]) · schema: identity · kycStatus · countryCode · expiry
✓ built · 200/200 tests passing · deployed Ethereum · Base · Arbitrum · Optimism

svg.text: NOT SHIBUI — downstream consumers. plug in via the standard interface.
⬜ not yet built — open work for the ecosystem
dashed border = out of Shibui scope
